sections.paragraph:
i.q. נוּשׂ Syr. ܢܳܫ (comp. Gr. νόσος) to be sick, ill at ease. It occurs only in Part. pass. אָנוּשׁ f. אֲנוּשָׁה sick, ill, used of a disease or wound, such as is scarcely curable, Jer 15:18 Mic 1:9 Job 34:6 of pain, Isa 17:11 (like חֹולֶה, נַחֲלֶה); of a calamitous day, Jer 17:16 of malignant disposition of mind, Jer 17:9.

Niphal, to be very ill, 2Sa 12:15.
